question: What is the purpose of this 22-study series on the Three Angels' Messages? How does it build on but go deeper than the Gospel Series (GSP), which proved the 3AM are the gospel? What is the relationship to the LGT, SV, and other series? Map the text of Rev 14:6-12 verse by verse and show how the 22 studies will cover every phrase, every theological connection, and every major objection. Include Rev 14:1-5 (preceding context) and Rev 14:13-20 (following context).

topics.name: GOSPEL
topics.entry_text: GOSPEL - Called: GOSPEL OF THE KINGDOM MAT 4:23; 24:14; GOSPEL OF GOD ROM 1:1; 15:16; 1TH 2:8; GOSPEL OF JESUS CHRIST MRK 1:1; GOSPEL OF CHRIST ROM 1:16; 1CO 9:12,18; GAL 1:7; GOSPEL OF SALVATION EPH 1:13; GOSPEL OF PEACE EPH 6:15. UNCLASSIFIED SCRIPTURES: ISA 40:8; MAT 4:23; 24:14; MRK 1:14,15; LUK 4:18,19; JHN 1:16,17; ACT 20:24; ROM 1:16,17; 10:15-18; 1CO 1:18; GAL 3:8; EPH 1:13,14; HEB 4:2; 1PE 1:23,25; REV 14:6,7. PROPHECIES RELATING TO: ISA 2:3-5; 9:2,6,7; 40:9; 52:7; 61:1-3; MAT 24:14.

topics.name: WORSHIP
topics.entry_text: WORSHIP — To be rendered only to God: EXO 20:3; DEU 5:7; 6:13; MAT 4:10; LUK 4:8; ACT 10:26; 14:15; COL 2:18; REV 19:10; 22:8. Loved by his people: PSA 27:4; 84:1-3,10. Postures in: Bowing EXO 34:8; 2CH 20:18. Prostration GEN 17:3; MRK 3:11.

topics.name: BABYLON
topics.entry_text: BABYLON — FIGURATIVE: REV 14:8; 16:19; 17; 18. City of: Built by Nimrod GEN 10:10. Capital of the kingdom DAN 4:30. Prophecies concerning: PSA 137:8,9; ISA 13; 14:4-26; 21:1-10; 47; JER 25:12-14; 50; 51; DAN 2:21-38; 5:25-29; 7.

topics.name: COMMANDMENTS
topics.entry_text: COMMANDMENTS — Referenced under LAW and DECALOGUE. Ecc 12:13: Fear God and keep his commandments. Rev 14:12: keep the commandments of God and the faith of Jesus. Rev 12:17: keep the commandments of God and have the testimony of Jesus Christ.

topics.name: FAITH
topics.entry_text: FAITH — IN CHRIST: General references including GAL 2:16; 3:22; ROM 3:22; PHP 3:9. Rev 14:12 'the faith of Jesus' — pistis Iesou genitive construction. Gen 15:6 Abraham believed and it was counted to him for righteousness.

topics.name: HARVEST
topics.entry_text: HARVEST, FIGURATIVE — JOL 3:13 Put ye in the sickle, for the harvest is ripe. MAT 13:39 The harvest is the end of the world. REV 14:15-16 grain harvest (therizo). REV 14:18-19 grape harvest (trygao). Dual-harvest eschatology.

topics.name: SEAL
topics.entry_text: SEAL, FIGURATIVE — REV 7:2-4 seal of the living God on foreheads of 144,000. REV 14:1 Father's name written in foreheads. EPH 1:13 sealed with the Holy Spirit of promise. Contrast with mark (charagma) of the beast in REV 13:16-17.

topics.name: IDOLATRY
topics.entry_text: IDOLATRY — DENUNCIATIONS AGAINST: EXO 20:3-5; DEU 5:7-9; 6:13-14. Beast worship as eschatological idolatry: REV 13:4,8,15; 14:9,11. Worship binary: Creator (Rev 14:7) vs. beast (Rev 14:9).

topics.name: FORNICATION
topics.entry_text: FORNICATION — Spiritual unfaithfulness (porneia G4202). Babylon's fornication: REV 14:8; 17:2,4; 18:3. Covenantal vocabulary from OT zanah (H2181) — requires prior covenant relationship. JER 51:7 golden cup imagery.

topics.name: GLORIFYING GOD
topics.entry_text: GLORIFYING GOD — COMMANDED: Rev 14:7 'give glory to him.' ISA 42:12 'Let them give glory unto the LORD.' JOS 7:19 'give glory to the LORD God of Israel.' 1CO 6:20 'glorify God in your body.' Nave's groups ISA 25:3, EZK 28:22, REV 14:7 under 'Glorifying God for his judgments.'

topics.name: SALVATION
topics.entry_text: SALVATION — GENERAL: ISA 52:7 good tidings of salvation. ROM 1:16 gospel is the power of God unto salvation. EPH 1:13 gospel of salvation. REV 14:6 everlasting gospel — the final proclamation of salvation to every nation.

topics.name: EVERLASTING LIFE
topics.entry_text: EVERLASTING LIFE — DAN 12:2 everlasting life/contempt. JHN 3:16 everlasting life. ROM 6:23 eternal life. REV 14:6 aionios modifying euangelion — the gospel characterized by its eternal nature.

topics.name: RIGHTEOUSNESS
topics.entry_text: RIGHTEOUSNESS BY FAITH — GEN 15:6 Abraham believed and it was counted to him for righteousness. ROM 3:22 righteousness by faith of Jesus Christ. ROM 4:3 quoted from Gen 15:6. GAL 3:6 same. REV 14:12 'the faith of Jesus' — the eschatological continuation of the righteousness-by-faith pattern.

topics.name: ANTICHRIST
topics.entry_text: ANTICHRIST — 2TH 2:3-4 man of sin, son of perdition. 1JN 2:18 antichrist. REV 13:1-10 beast from sea. REV 13:11-18 beast from earth. The three angels' messages directly counter the beast's counterfeit worship system.

topics.name: WRATH
topics.entry_text: WRATH — Two Greek terms in Rev 14: thumos (G2372, turbulent passionate fury) in Rev 14:8,10,19 and orge (G3709, settled judicial wrath) in Rev 14:10. Cup of wrath tradition: PSA 75:8; ISA 51:17; JER 25:15-16.

verses.heading: The Lamb on Mount Zion with the 144,000 — preceding context establishing the narrative frame
verses.kjv_text: And I looked, and, lo, a Lamb stood on the mount Sion, and with him an hundred forty [and] four thousand, having his Father's name written in their foreheads.
verses.context: Rev 14 opens with a vision of the Lamb (arnion G721) standing on Mount Zion with the 144,000. This follows Rev 13's description of the beast system. The Father's name in foreheads directly contrasts with the beast's mark (Rev 13:16-17). This is the literary frame for the three angels' messages.

greek_parsing.gloss: εἶδον,               ὁράω            G3708    [V-2AAI-1S] Aor Act Ind Sg firstPers
Perf Act Ptcp Nom Sg N
τὸ                   ὁ               G3588    [T-ASN] Acc Sg N
Pres Act Ptcp Nom Pl F
Perf Pass Ptcp Acc Sg N

verses.notes: Key contrasts: Father's name (14:1) vs. beast's mark (13:16); Lamb standing (hestos, perfect active participle of histemi — settled, permanent position) on Zion vs. beast rising from sea (13:1). The seal/mark contrast is established before the three angels speak. Prior study rev14-144000-sequence established the heavenly setting (throne, four beasts, elders in 14:3). Prior study mark-of-beast-vs-seal-of-god established seal = forehead only (conviction) vs. mark = forehead or hand (conformity or compliance).

verses.heading: The new song of the 144,000 — supporting context
verses.kjv_text: And I heard a voice from heaven, as the voice of many waters, and as the voice of a great thunder: and I heard the voice of harpers harping with their harps: And they sung as it were a new song before the throne, and before the four beasts, and the elders: and no man could learn that song but the hundred [and] forty [and] four thousand, which were redeemed from the earth.
verses.context: The 144,000 sing a unique song before the heavenly throne. They are 'redeemed from the earth' — using agorazo (G59, to purchase), the same commercial metaphor as Rev 5:9. The exclusivity of the song indicates unique experience.
verses.notes: Supporting context verse. The heavenly setting (throne, four beasts, elders) confirms this is a heavenly scene, not earthly. The 144,000 are already with the Lamb, functioning as firstfruits (14:4).

verses.heading: The 144,000 described — firstfruits, virgins, without fault
verses.kjv_text: These are they which were not defiled with women; for they are virgins. These are they which follow the Lamb whithersoever he goeth. These were redeemed from among men, [being] the firstfruits unto God and to the Lamb. And in their mouth was found no guile: for they are without fault before the throne of God.
verses.context: Three descriptors of the 144,000: (1) spiritual purity (virgins, not defiled); (2) firstfruits (aparche G536) — the first portion of the harvest pledging the full harvest to come; (3) without fault (amomoi G299) — OT sacrificial purity language (unblemished offering). The LGT series directly connects to 14:5's 'without fault' claim.

greek_parsing.gloss: γυναικῶν             γυνή            G1135    [N-GPF] Gen Pl F
ἐμολύνθησαν·         μολύνω          G3435    [V-API-3P] Aor Pass Ind Pl thirdPers
εἰσιν.               εἰμί            G1510    [V-PAI-3P] Pres Act Ind Pl thirdPers
Pres Act Ptcp Nom Pl M
τῶν                  ὁ               G3588    [T-GPM] Gen Pl M
τῷ                   ὁ               G3588    [T-DSN] Dat Sg N

verses.notes: aparche (G536) connects to the grain harvest of 14:14-16 — the firstfruits guarantee a positive full harvest. amomoi (G299) is sacrificial vocabulary — the word used for unblemished offerings in the LXX. pseudos (G5579, lie/guile) absent from their mouth connects to Rev 21:27 and 22:15 where liars are excluded. Prior study rev14-144000-sequence established the firstfruits-harvest typological connection.

verses.heading: FIRST ANGEL — the everlasting gospel proclaimed to every nation
verses.kjv_text: And I saw another angel fly in the midst of heaven, having the everlasting gospel to preach unto them that dwell on the earth, and to every nation, and kindred, and tongue, and people,
verses.context: The first angel carries euangelion aionion — the only occurrence of 'everlasting gospel' in all of Scripture. The anarthrous construction (no definite article) is qualitative, emphasizing the character of the gospel rather than distinguishing it from another. The four-fold audience (ethnos, phyle, glossa, laos) matches the Danielic pattern of Dan 7:14. This verse is the structural beginning of the three angels' messages.

greek_parsing.gloss: εἶδον                ὁράω            G3708    [V-2AAI-1S] Aor Act Ind Sg firstPers
Pres M/P Ptcp Acc Sg M
μεσουρανήματι,       μεσουράνημα     G3321    [N-DSN] Dat Sg N
Pres Act Ptcp Acc Sg M
τοὺς                 ὁ               G3588    [T-APM] Acc Pl M
Pres M/P Ptcp Acc Pl M

verses.notes: euangelion (G2098) occurs only once in Revelation — here. aionios (G166) + euangelion is a hapax pairing in all Scripture. euangelisai (aorist active infinitive of euangelizo G2097) connects to OT basar (H1319) through the tightest verb-to-verb LXX bridge (PMI 29.44 per G2097 library entry). mesouranema (G3321) = mid-heaven, the zenith — maximum visibility. The four-fold audience formula overlaps with Rev 10:11 (prophesy again to peoples, nations, tongues, kings) — three of four terms match, confirming the 'prophesy again' content IS the three angels' messages (sv-21).

verses.heading: FIRST ANGEL — Fear God, give glory, hour of judgment, worship the Creator
verses.kjv_text: Saying with a loud voice, Fear God, and give glory to him; for the hour of his judgment is come: and worship him that made heaven, and earth, and the sea, and the fountains of waters.
verses.context: The legon (present active participle of lego G3004) introduces the direct discourse of the euangelisai from v.6 — the commands ARE the gospel's own words. Three aorist imperatives: phobethete (fear!), dote (give glory!), proskynesate (worship!) — decisive, crisis-moment acts. The hoti clause grounds the commands: 'the hour of his judgment HAS COME' (elthen, aorist = accomplished fact). The creation formula directly echoes Exo 20:11.

greek_parsing.gloss: Pres Act Ptcp Nom Sg M
φωνῇ                 φωνή            G5456    [N-DSF] Dat Sg F
δότε                 δίδωμι          G1325    [V-2AAM-2P] Aor Act Impv Pl secondPers
ἦλθεν                ἔρχομαι         G2064    [V-2AAI-3S] Aor Act Ind Sg thirdPers
Aor Act Ptcp Dat Sg M

verses.notes: Structural parallel with Eccl 12:13-14: fear God + commandments + judgment. Prior study gsp-10 verified the Exo 20:11 creation formula (poieo/asah, highest-confidence LXX pair, 2022 co-occurrences). The addition of 'fountains of waters' (pegas hydaton G4077) beyond Exo 20:11's formula may echo Gen 7:11 (fountains of the great deep) or broaden the creation scope. elthen (aorist of erchomai G2064) treats the judgment arrival as fait accompli — not 'is coming' but 'has come.' hora (G5610) with the definite article = THE appointed hour. krisis (G2920) = judicial process, not condemnation (katakrima G2631). LXX chain: dina (Dan 7:10) → mishpat (H4941) → krisis (Rev 14:7). proskyneo (G4352) appears on BOTH sides of the conflict: 14:7 (worship Creator), 14:9,11 (worship beast) — worship is the central unifying theme.

verses.heading: SECOND ANGEL — Babylon is fallen, wine of her fornication
verses.kjv_text: And there followed another angel, saying, Babylon is fallen, is fallen, that great city, because she made all nations drink of the wine of the wrath of her fornication.
verses.context: The second angel's message directly quotes Isa 21:9 (naphlah naphlah babel). The doubled aorist (epesen epesen) is prophetic aorist — treating future judgment as already accomplished. pepotiken (perfect active indicative of potizo G4222) shows completed action with lasting results: the nations HAVE BEEN made drunk. The genitive chain: wine → thumos → porneia → hers.

greek_parsing.gloss: ἄλλος                ἄλλος           G243     [A-NSM] Nom Sg M
Pres Act Ptcp Nom Sg M
τοῦ                  ὁ               G3588    [T-GSM] Gen Sg M

verses.notes: OT composite: Isa 21:9 ('Babylon is fallen, is fallen') + Jer 51:7 ('golden cup in the LORD's hand that made all the earth drunken'). porneia (G4202) is covenantal vocabulary from OT zanah (H2181) — requires prior covenant relationship; Babylon is apostate religion, not paganism. Prior study rev-14-babylon-three-angels established the mother-daughter structure (Rev 17:5) and 'my people' inside Babylon (Rev 18:4). thumos (G2372) here modifies Babylon's wine, not God's — the polarity flips in v.10 where God's wine (thumos + orge) is served. Grammar note: prophetic aorist treats the fall as certain though not yet fully visible — see grammar reference greek/tense-aorist-prophetic.md.

verses.heading: THIRD ANGEL — beast worship, mark, God's unmixed wrath, fire and brimstone
verses.kjv_text: And the third angel followed them, saying with a loud voice, If any man worship the beast and his image, and receive [his] mark in his forehead, or in his hand, The same shall drink of the wine of the wrath of God, which is poured out without mixture into the cup of his indignation; and he shall be tormented with fire and brimstone in the presence of the holy angels, and in the presence of the Lamb:
verses.context: The third angel's warning is the most solemn in Scripture. Ei tis (if anyone) + present indicative (proskunei, lambanei) = open condition with present tense, describing an ongoing choice. Two wrath words: thumos (G2372, turbulent passionate fury — the wine's content) and orge (G3709, settled judicial wrath — the cup's nature). kekerasmenou akratou = 'mixed unmixed' — oxymoronic intensifier meaning undiluted, full-strength. Fire and brimstone echoes Gen 19:24-25 (Sodom).

greek_parsing.gloss: ἄλλος                ἄλλος           G243     [A-NSM] Nom Sg M
Pres Act Ptcp Nom Sg M
φωνῇ                 φωνή            G5456    [N-DSF] Dat Sg F
τις                  τὶς             G5100    [X-NSM] Nom Sg M
τοῦ                  ὁ               G3588    [T-GSN] Gen Sg N

verses.notes: kekerasmenou (perfect passive participle of kerannymi G2767) + akratou (G194, unmixed/undiluted) = wine prepared at full strength without dilution. poterion (G4221) of orge (G3709) = the cup of settled judicial wrath. basanisthesetai (future passive indicative of basanizo G928) — related to basanismos (G929, torment). theion (G2303, brimstone/sulfur) echoes Gen 19:24. The Lamb (arnion G721) is present during judgment — the same Lamb who stands with the 144,000 in 14:1. The proskyneo binary is complete: worship the Creator (14:7) → OR → face divine wrath for worshiping the beast (14:9-10).

verses.heading: THIRD ANGEL continued — smoke ascending, no rest, the forever language
verses.kjv_text: And the smoke of their torment ascendeth up for ever and ever: and they have no rest day nor night, who worship the beast and his image, and whosoever receiveth the mark of his name.
verses.context: basanismos (G929, torment/testing) — the smoke ascends as evidence of completed judgment. 'For ever and ever' (eis aionas aionon) resolved by Isa 34:10 pattern: permanent results, not endless conscious process. 'No rest' (ouk echousin anapausin G372) contrasts directly with 14:13 ('rest from labours'). Present tenses (anabainei, echousin, proskynountes) describe ongoing condition.

greek_parsing.gloss: ὁ                    ὁ               G3588    [T-NSM] Nom Sg M
αἰῶνας               αἰών            G165     [N-APM] Acc Pl M
Pres Act Ptcp Nom Pl M

verses.notes: Prior study for-ever-and-ever-revelation established: when applied to judgment (Rev 14:11, 19:3, 20:10), 'for ever and ever' describes permanence of destruction, not endless burning — Isaiah 34:10 uses identical language for Edom, which is not burning today; Jude 1:7 calls Sodom's fire 'eternal' but 2 Pet 2:6 says it reduced them to ashes. The anapausis/anapauō contrast (14:11 vs. 14:13) is built on Sabbath-rest theology — Vos claims beast-worshipers 'never attain the seventh day.' Heb 4:9 (sabbatismos) bridges creation rest to eschatological rest. The proskynountes (present active participle, 'those who worship') describes the ongoing character of beast-worshipers.

verses.heading: The saints described — patience, commandments of God, faith of Jesus
verses.kjv_text: Here is the patience of the saints: here [are] they that keep the commandments of God, and the faith of Jesus.
verses.context: The summary verse of the three angels' messages. Three elements converge: (1) hupomone (G5281, endurance under pressure — active resistance, not passive waiting); (2) terountes tas entolas tou Theou (present active participle, 'those keeping THE commandments of GOD' — specific, divine law); (3) ten pistin Iesou ('the faith of Jesus' — genitive construction admitting both subjective and objective readings). This verse synthesizes the entire passage.

greek_parsing.gloss: ἡ                    ὁ               G3588    [T-NSF] Nom Sg F
Pres Act Ptcp Nom Pl M
τὴν                  ὁ               G3588    [T-ASF] Acc Sg F

verses.notes: hupomone inclusio: Rev 13:10 ('here is the patience and the faith of the saints') → Rev 14:12 (expanded). terountes (present active participle of tereo G5083) describes, does not prescribe — it identifies a group by their habitual action, not commanding that action. The parallel with Rev 12:17 ('keep the commandments of God and have the testimony of Jesus Christ') creates a structural bridge — the remnant defined twice by the same formula. pistis Iesou: subjective genitive ('Jesus' own faithfulness') parallels 'commandments of God' (God is source of commandments, Jesus is source of faith). Both readings produce the same practical outcome. The Eccl 12:13-14 triad is complete: fear God (14:7) + commandments (14:12) + judgment (14:7). Prior study gsp-10 established five lines proving 14:12 describes gospel RESULTS, not additional requirements.

verses.heading: Beatitude — blessed are the dead who die in the Lord, rest from labours
verses.kjv_text: And I heard a voice from heaven saying unto me, Write, Blessed [are] the dead which die in the Lord from henceforth: Yea, saith the Spirit, that they may rest from their labours; and their works do follow them.
verses.context: The second beatitude in Revelation (cf. Rev 1:3). makarios (G3107, blessed) pronounced on those dying 'in the Lord' — aparti (from now on) marks a specific temporal point. anapahesontai (future passive of anapauo G373) = 'they shall rest' — God grants the rest. Direct contrast with 14:11's 'no rest.' The Spirit confirms the blessing.
